description_long: This object consists of two adjacent railway crossing signals, each mounted on a vertical pole with a round base. At the top of each pole is a crossbuck sign, one with yellow and black stripes and the other with white and black stripes, indicating a railroad crossing. Below the crossbucks are two large, round signal lights facing forward, and above each crossbuck is a small, dome-shaped warning light. Each pole also features a rectangular control box with red text, and the poles themselves are striped in black and white or black and yellow. The overall construction appears robust, with metal components and plastic or glass lenses for the lights. The design is typical for railway crossings in urban environments, providing clear visual and illuminated warnings to both vehicles and pedestrians.
description: A pair of pole-mounted railway crossing signals with crossbuck signs, dual signal lights, and warning beacons, featuring black/yellow and black/white color schemes.
description_view_0: Front view showing both railway crossing signals, their crossbuck signs, dual round signal lights, and control boxes with red text.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the signal lights, the protruding warning beacons, and the striped poles.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the crossbuck signs, the rear of the signal lights, and the control boxes.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the side profile of the yellow/black striped pole and the arrangement of the lights and beacons.
